touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Sophia Kelly introducing onboarding automation solution. No response received.
LinkedIn connection request and brief message sent to Sophia Kelly. No response received.
Follow-up email highlighting ROI from onboarding automation for fintech companies. Sophia responded showing interest in learning more.

questions_asked.question: What kind of results have you seen with other fintech companies?
questions_asked.answer: Our fintech clients typically see 40-60% reduction in onboarding time and 3x faster time-to-productivity for new hires.

touches.ai_summary: Attempted cold call to Sophia Kelly. Call went to voicemail, left brief message referencing email exchange.
Email proposing times for discovery call. Sophia confirmed availability and mentioned they're currently evaluating solutions including Gong.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the evaluation right now?
questions_asked.answer: We're scaling the sales team from 12 to 25 reps this quarter and onboarding is a bottleneck.

next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent with case study relevant to fintech onboarding challenges. No response received.
Pre-call email with discovery agenda. Sophia confirmed and asked to include use case examples specific to RevOps workflows.
Qualification call with Sophia Kelly covering current onboarding process, pain points, and timeline. Confirmed 19-seat opportunity with unlimited automation tier interest.

questions_asked.question: Walk me through your current onboarding process for new sales hires.
questions_asked.answer: It's mostly manual - 2 weeks of shadowing, scattered documentation, and RevOps spends about 8 hours per new hire getting them set up in our systems.

questions_asked.question: How does your automation compare to what Gong offers for onboarding?
questions_asked.answer: Gong focuses primarily on call recording and coaching. Our platform automates the entire onboarding workflow including system provisioning, content sequencing, and progress tracking.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call to discuss technical requirements and integration needs. Sophia confirmed they use Salesforce and Slack, asked about implementation timeline.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline for a company our size?
questions_asked.answer: For 19 seats with your tech stack, usually 3-4 weeks from kickoff to first cohort onboarded.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this solution?
questions_asked.answer: Quinn Chen, our COO, will need to sign off. Zainab Katz from our team will handle the procurement side.

touches.ai_summary: Email recapping discovery insights and sharing demo agenda. Sophia confirmed and requested focus on ROI metrics and reporting capabilities.
Pre-demo email with personalized agenda based on CoastalStudio's fintech use case. Sophia confirmed attendance and mentioned Gong demo is scheduled for same week.
Product demo focused on onboarding automation workflows, Salesforce integration, and analytics dashboard. Sophia engaged positively but asked detailed questions about pricing flexibility.

questions_asked.question: Can you show me how the analytics compare to what we'd get with Gong's onboarding features?
questions_asked.answer: Demonstrated side-by-side comparison showing our workflow completion tracking, time-to-productivity metrics, and manager oversight dashboard.

questions_asked.question: What does the unlimited automation tier actually include versus the standard tier?
questions_asked.answer: Unlimited tier removes caps on workflow complexity, allows custom integrations, and includes advanced AI-powered content recommendations.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: We need something in place before Q2 hiring starts, so ideally signed by end of month.

objections: Need to understand total cost of ownership compared to Gong's bundled pricing

touches.ai_summary: Post-demo email with proposal outline and ROI calculator. Sophia acknowledged and asked for more competitive pricing given they're evaluating Gong.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on pricing if we commit to annual contract?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can discuss volume and commitment discounts on our proposal call.

touches.ai_summary: Scheduled proposal call with Sophia Kelly was rescheduled last minute due to conflict on her side.
Proposal call presenting $13,800 ACV for 19 seats with unlimited automation tier. Sophia expressed concern about budget and mentioned Gong's pricing is more competitive.

questions_asked.question: Gong quoted us about 30% less for similar seat count - can you get closer to that?
questions_asked.answer: Our value proposition is different given the onboarding-specific features, but I can explore discount options for annual commitment.

questions_asked.question: What would it take to get this over the line by month end?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, pricing is the main blocker. If you can get within 15-20% of Gong's quote, I can push this through.

questions_asked.question: Can we start with standard tier and upgrade later?
questions_asked.answer: We could, but based on your workflow requirements from discovery, you'd hit the limits within first quarter.

objections: Pricing significantly higher than Gong's competing offer
Budget constraints given expansion hiring plans
next_steps: Loop in COO for executive alignment

touches.ai_summary: Call with Quinn Chen (COO) to discuss strategic value and ROI. Quinn supportive of onboarding solution but deferred pricing approval to Sophia and procurement team.

questions_asked.question: What's your biggest concern around the scaling challenge this quarter?
questions_asked.answer: Time to productivity. We can't afford new reps ramping for 6-8 weeks when we need them selling in 3-4 weeks.

questions_asked.question: Have you worked with other fintech companies at our stage?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, several in 400-600 employee range. Typical ROI is 4-5x in first year from reduced ramp time alone.

next_steps: Sophia to finalize terms with procurement

touches.ai_summary: Pricing negotiation call with Sophia Kelly. Presented 21.7% discount bringing ACV to $13,800, positioning it as maximum flexibility given annual commit and fintech focus.

questions_asked.question: Is this the best you can do, or is there more room?
questions_asked.answer: This is at the top end of our approval range. I had to get VP sign-off for this level of discount.

questions_asked.question: Does this pricing work within your budget parameters?
questions_asked.answer: It's closer. I need to validate with Zainab on the procurement side, but this feels more workable.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we need to add seats mid-year?
questions_asked.answer: Additional seats would be pro-rated at the same discounted per-seat rate for remainder of contract term.

objections: Still concerned about year-over-year cost compared to Gong's multi-year lock-in pricing

touches.ai_summary: Email with updated contract reflecting 21.7% discount and payment terms. Sophia acknowledged and said procurement team is reviewing alongside Gong's final offer.
Negotiation call addressing final concerns around contract terms, implementation support, and quarterly business reviews. Sophia warmer but non-committal pending final internal discussion.

questions_asked.question: Can we include implementation support in the base price rather than as add-on?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can bundle 40 hours of implementation support at no additional cost given the deal size.

questions_asked.question: What's the internal timeline for final decision?
questions_asked.answer: Zainab needs to present to finance by Friday. We should have answer early next week.

objections: Want guarantee of quarterly executive business reviews given the investment
next_steps: Final decision by early next week

touches.ai_summary: Final pricing confirmation call. Sophia confirmed pricing works, implementation support bundle appreciated. Waiting on Zainab's final procurement sign-off and Quinn's contract review.

questions_asked.question: When can we realistically start implementation if we sign this week?
questions_asked.answer: We can kick off within 5 business days of signed contract. First onboarding cohort could go live in 3-4 weeks.

questions_asked.question: Anything else blocking on your side?
questions_asked.answer: No, just final approvals. I'm confident we'll move forward - this solves our immediate need better than the alternatives.

next_steps: Await final signatures from Quinn and procurement
